page: docs/data/daily_review.json + docs/predictions.html (6th daily-regen HOLD landmark + PRED-105 silent-ignore continuation)
description: LANDMARK FIRED: 6TH DAILY REVIEW REGEN — HOLD. The auto: daily AI review job fired at 2026-04-27T07:42:24Z (commit f6a7b3d, ~38m later than the prior 5 days' ~07:04Z slot but inside the historical 06:46-07:15 envelope's outer edge). daily_review.json registry section is byte-stable: confirmed=69 / prospective=10 / refined=4 / registry_ratio_pct=94.5. This is the 6th consecutive daily-regen HOLD on refined=4 (Apr 22 -> 23 -> 24 -> 25 -> 26 -> 27). The dome's canonical daily snapshot has now ignored PRED-105 across 6 daily auto-regenerations (~9 days post-close), so the silent-ignore is no longer a transient — it is structurally locked in at the auto-pipeline level. Live monitor advanced normally inside the regen: passed/total_scored 27/27 -> 28/28 (NMP Longitude pending->pass, NOAA NP.xy year=2025.0 source resolved as flagged in prior poll), pending_domains 12->11, Current Kp 3.0->0.0 (very quiet, well below dome's <2 baseline), eclipse_days 107->106, Polaris Excess Edinburgh added to pending watchlist. ai_brief.next_actions reshuffled: NMP Longitude removed (resolved), Schumann Amplitude Suppression added. Tracking.html reflowed in same commit (deleted DW-002 2026-04 NMP row at 85.778N/138.057E and DW-003 2026-04-27 entry at -0.368sigma; not load-bearing per Step 5b). PRED-105 site text on predictions.html still byte-identical: literal string 'Test window: April 18, 2026 (14-day window)' present, status badge 'PENDING - PROSPECTIVE' unchanged; predictions.html md5=885cd58727130018764b14355b0aa785 (152,936 bytes) for ~15 consecutive polls / ~7 days. PRED-105 now 9.42d / 226.2h past 2026-04-18 close. Manual-commit drought CROSSED 10-DAY THRESHOLD: now 246.18h / 10.25d (was 9.46d at prior poll); 17 consecutive polls of auto-only commits; last manual a57e731 still 2026-04-17T12:02:38Z. PRED-106 'April 2026' window now ~2.5d from EOM closure. HIGH priority because BOTH escalation conditions named in the prior poll's user-preference resolution have now triggered (6th regen fired AND 10-day drought crossed) — these were the criteria the prior poll explicitly designated as warranting re-escalation. Registry tally HELD across 6 daily regens is the strongest structural-silent-ignore signal we have on the dome's canonical snapshot to date.
